questions.text: Which of the following compounds does not exhibit positional isomerism?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Alkynes are hydrocarbons which contain carbon-carbon triple bonds. Their general formula is CnH2n-2 for molecules with one triple bond (and no rings). Alkynes undergo many of the same reactions as alkenes, but can react twice because of the presence of the two p-bonds in the triple bond.
The polar structure of nitro alkanes is supported by their large dipole moment. The resonance hybrid structure suggests that the two N−O bonds are equal.

A carboxylic acid is an organic compound that contains the carboxyl functional group. The general formula for a carboxylic acid can be abbreviated as R−COOH. The carbon atom of the carboxyl group may be attached to a hydrogen atom or to a carbon chain.
The functional group of an alcohol is the hydroxyl group, –OH. Unlike the alkyl halides, this group has two reactive covalent bonds, the C–O bond and the O–H bond.

questions.explanation: In carboxylic acids position of functional group does not change therefore they do not show positional isomerism.

questions.text: Tetraethyl lead is added to fuel to _?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Tetraethyl lead is not used to alter the boiling point of fuel. Instead, it serves a different function related to engine performance.
While overheating is a concern in engines, tetraethyl lead is not used to address this issue directly. It has a different purpose in fuel enhancement.
Fuel freezing is not a common issue that tetraethyl lead addresses. Its role is more specific to engine performance under combustion.
Tetraethyl lead is added to fuel primarily as an antiknock agent. It increases the octane number, thereby preventing engine knocking and improving performance.

questions.explanation: Tetraethyllead was historically used as an anti-knock agent in gasoline. It increases the fuel's octane rating, which prevents premature ignition (knocking or pinging) in high-compression internal combustion engines.

questions.text: Which of the following is an amide?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This option is correct. H2NCONH2 is the formula of urea and it belongs to class amide.
This option is incorrect. NH2CH3 is an amine known as methanamine.
This option is incorrect. C6H6NH2 is the formula of amino benzene also known as aniline.
This option is incorrect. This is trimethylamine which is an organic compound and belongs to class of amine.

questions.explanation: Urea is organic compound which belongs to amide class

questions.text: Isomerism due to shifting of proton from one atom to another in a same molecule is known as:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Metamerism is the type of isomerism in which chemical compounds have identical proportions of the same elements and the same molecular weight, but have radicals differing in type or position, with resulting differences in chemical properties.

Tautomerism is the existence of two or more chemical compounds that are capable of facile inter-conversion, in many cases merely exchanging a hydrogen atom between two other atoms, to either of which it forms a covalent bond.

Positional isomers are constitutional isomers with the same carbon skeleton and will have the same functional group. They differ from each other in the position of the functional groups on the carbon chain and the phenomenon is known as positional isomerism.

Functional Isomerism is also known as functional group isomerism. As the name suggests, it refers to the compounds that have the same chemical formula but different functional groups attached to them.

questions.explanation: A tautomer of a compound refers to the isomer of the compound which only differs in the position of protons and electrons so, B is correct.In the rest of the options (A, C, and D), there is a difference in the positioning of whole atoms and not just protons, which is why these options are incorrect.

questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: % of s character =1/3 x 100=33.3%
In sp2 hybrid orbital% of s character =1/3 x 100=33.3%
% of s character =1/3 x 100=33.3%
% of s character =1/3 x 100=33.3%

questions.explanation: In sp2 hybrid orbital% of s character =1/3 x 100=33.3%In sp3% of p character = 3/4 x 100 = 75%

questions.text: Total number of possible isomers of butyl alcohol among alcohols are:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Butanol (also called butyl alcohol) is a four-carbon alcohol with a formula of C4H9OH, which occurs in five isomeric structures (four structural isomers), from a straight-chain primary alcohol to a branched-chain tertiary alcohol; all are a butyl or isobutyl group linked to a hydroxyl group.

Butanol (also called butyl alcohol) is a four-carbon alcohol with a formula of C4H9OH, which occurs in five isomeric structures (four structural isomers), from a straight-chain primary alcohol to a branched-chain tertiary alcohol; all are a butyl or isobutyl group linked to a hydroxyl group.

Butanol (also called butyl alcohol) is a four-carbon alcohol with a formula of C4H9OH, which occurs in five isomeric structures (four structural isomers), from a straight-chain primary alcohol to a branched-chain tertiary alcohol; all are a butyl or isobutyl group linked to a hydroxyl group.

Butanol (also called butyl alcohol) is a four-carbon alcohol with a formula of C4H9OH, which occurs in five isomeric structures (four structural isomers), from a straight-chain primary alcohol to a branched-chain tertiary alcohol; all are a butyl or isobutyl group linked to a hydroxyl group.

questions.explanation: Butanol (also called butyl alcohol) is a four-carbon alcohol with a formula of C4H9OH, which occurs in five isomeric structures (four structural isomers), from a straight-chain primary alcohol to a branched-chain tertiary alcohol; all are a butyl or isobutyl group linked to a hydroxyl group.

questions.text: IUPAC name of C6H5 - CH2 - CH2 - NH2 is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The parent hydrocarbon is ethane. The substituent (phenyl group) is present at a second carbon atom.Hence, the IUPAC name is 2-phenyl ethanamine.

2-phenyl ethanamide, also known as phenylacetamide, is an organic compound with the chemical formula C8H9NO. It is classified as an amide and consists of a phenyl group (C6H5) attached to the amino group (NH2) of an ethanamide (CH3CONH) molecule.Phenylacetamide is a white crystalline solid that is sparingly soluble in water. It is primarily used as an intermediate in the synthesis of various pharmaceuticals, agrochemicals, and other organic compounds. It can be chemically modified to produce a wide range of derivatives with different properties and applications.
The parent hydrocarbon is ethane. The substituent (phenyl group) is present at a second carbon atom. Hence, the IUPAC name is 2-phenyl ethanamine.

3-phenyl ethanamine: This name is incorrect because "3-phenyl" indicates the presence of a phenyl group attached to the third carbon atom of the parent chain. However, in the given compound, the phenyl group is attached to the first carbon atom.

questions.explanation: The compound C6H5−CH2−CH2−NH2 is an amine. The parent hydrocarbon is ethane. The substituent (phenyl group) is present at a second carbon atom.Hence, the IUPAC name is 2-phenylethanamine.

questions.text: The type of structural isomerism which arises due to the difference in the nature of carbon chain or carbon skeleton is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Chain isomerism type of isomerism arises due to the difference in the nature of the carbon chain.
Position isomerism type of isomerism arises due to the difference in the position of the same functional group on the carbon chain. The arrangement of carbon atoms remains the same.

Such compounds which possess the same structural formula, but differ with respect to the positions of the identical groups in space are called cistrans isomers and the phenomenon is known as the cis-trans or geometric isomerism.

An optically active compounds exists in two isomeric forms that rotate the plane polarized light in opposite directions. They are called optical isomers and the phenomena is called optical isomerism. The optical rotatory power of two isomers are equal in magnitude. but opposite in direction.

questions.explanation: Chain isomerism is a type of isomerism arises due to the difference in the nature of the carbon chain.

questions.text: 1-Chloropropane and 2-Chloropropane are _?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Functional group isomers have the same molecular formula but different functional groups. 1-Chloropropane and 2-Chloropropane have the same functional group-a halogen.
Chain isomers have the same molecular formula but different carbon skeletons. 1-Chloropropane and 2-Chloropropane have the same carbon skeleton.
The position of the chlorine atom in 1-Chloropropane and 2-Chloropropane is different, so they are Position Isomers.

Metamers have the same molecular formula but different alkyl groups attached to the same functional group. 1-Chloropropane and 2-Chloropropane do not have the same alkyl groups attached to the same functional group.

questions.explanation: The position of the chlorine atom in 1-Chloropropane and 2-Chloropropane is different, so they are Position Isomers.

questions.text: The number of chiral centres in a molecule 5-bromo 3-chloro hexan-2-ol is/are:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: If there is only one chiral center in the molecule, it means there is only one carbon atom with four different substituents attached to it. A chiral center is also known as a stereocenter, and its presence indicates that the molecule is chiral and can have enantiomers (mirror-image isomers).

A chiral centre is an atom especially in an organic molecule that has four unique atoms or groups attached to it. To 3 carbons three different functional groups are attached. Therefore, 3 chiral centres are present.Note. To other adjacent carbon don't confuse the fact that 2 H are attached irrespective of different functional groups so they can't be considered chiral.
If there are two chiral centers in the molecule, it means there are two carbon atoms, each with four different substituents attached.
If there are five chiral centers in the molecule, it means there are five carbon atoms, each with four different substituents attached.

questions.explanation: A chiral centre is an atom especially in an organic molecule that has four unique atoms or groups attached to it. To 3 carbons three different functional groups are attached. Therefore, 3 chiral centers are present.Note. To other adjacent carbon don't confuse the fact that 2 H are attached irrespective of different functional groups so they can’t be considered chiral.

questions.text: Which of the following shows tautomerism?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Amino acids can exhibit tautomerism due to the presence of an alpha hydrogen on the carbon adjacent to the amino group. This allows for the proton to shift between different atoms in the molecule, leading to different structural forms.

Amines lack the structural requirements for tautomerism as they do not have an alpha hydrogen on a carbon adjacent to a functional group that can accept or donate a proton, making them incapable of exhibiting this phenomenon.

While carboxylic acids do contain functional groups that can interact with protons, they do not exhibit tautomerism in the same manner as amino acids because they lack the necessary alpha hydrogen in the correct structural context for proton migration.
This option is incorrect because not all listed compounds exhibit tautomerism. Amino acids are the only ones that satisfy the required conditions for tautomerism.

questions.explanation: Tautomerism is a form of isomerism involving the migration of a proton. The most common type, keto-enol tautomerism, requires the presence of a hydrogen atom on a carbon adjacent (alpha) to a carbonyl group.

questions.text: Which among the following does not exhibit geometric isomerism?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The formula of 1-hexene is Ch3-CH2-CH2-CH2-CH=CH2.The one carbon atom bearing a double bond is attached to two H atoms.Hence,it does not exhibit geometric isomerism.
In 2-hexene, the carbon atoms bear double bonds attachedbto two different atoms.Hence,it exhibits geometrical isomerism.
Two geometrical isomers can be possible for 3-hexene. .

Geometric isomerism is exhibited by those compounds that consist of two carbon atoms joined by a double bond and have two distinct functional groups on either side of the double bond thus 4-hexene exhibits geometrical isomerism.

questions.explanation: The formula of 1-hexene is Ch3-CH2-CH2-CH2-CH=CH2.The one carbon atom bearing a double bond is attached to two H atoms.Hence,it does not exhibit geometric isomerism.

questions.text: Alicyclic organic compounds belong to:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Heterocyclic compounds are organic compounds that contain a ring structure with at least one atom that is not carbon. These atoms can be nitrogen, oxygen, sulfur, or other elements. They add diversity to the structure of organic compounds.

An alicyclic compound is defined as an organic compound, which is both cyclic and aliphatic. They either have one or more all-carbon rings that may be either saturated or unsaturated, but they do not hold aromatic character. Alicyclic compounds can have either one or more aliphatic side chains attached
Saturated compounds are organic compounds that contain only single bonds between carbon atoms. They are "saturated" with hydrogen atoms.
Aromatic compounds are a special type of organic compounds that contain a ring of atoms with alternating double bonds. They often have a pleasant smell, hence the name "aromatic."

questions.explanation: An alicyclic compound is defined as an organic compound, which is both cyclic and aliphatic. They either have one or more all-carbon rings that may be either saturated or unsaturated, but they do not hold aromatic character. Alicyclic compounds can have either one or more aliphatic side chains attached.

questions.text: Fuels with higher octane number can be produced by _.
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Cracking involves breaking down large hydrocarbons into smaller ones and does not necessarily result in a higher octane number.

Catalytic reforming is a chemical process used to convert petroleum refinery naphthas distilled from crude oil (typically having low octane ratings) into high-octane liquid products called reformates, which are premium blending stocks for high-octane gasoline. The process converts low-octane linear hydrocarbons (paraffins) into branched alkanes (isoparaffins) and cyclic naphthenes, which are then partially dehydrogenated to produce high- octane aromatic hydrocarbons.
Decomposition is a general term that can refer to any process in which a compound is broken down into simpler components. It does not necessarily result in a higher octane number.

Isomerisation is a process in which straight-chain hydrocarbons are converted into their isomers, which have a more branched and compact structure. While this can result in a higher octane number, it is not as effective as reforming in producing high-octane fuels.

questions.explanation: Option A) Cracking involves breaking down large hydrocarbons into smaller ones and does not necessarily result in a higher octane number.Option B: Catalytic reforming is a chemical process used to convert petroleum refinery naphthas distilled from crude oil (typically having low octane ratings) into high-octane liquid products called reformates, which are premium blending stocks for high-octane gasoline. The process converts low-octane linear hydrocarbons (paraffin) into branched alkanes (isoparaffins) and cyclic naphthenes, which are then partially dehydrogenated to produce high-octane aromatic hydrocarbons. Option C) Decomposition is a general term that can refer to any process in which a compound is broken down into simpler components. It does not necessarily result in a higher octane number.Option D) Isomerisation is a process in which straight-chain hydrocarbons are converted into their isomers, which have a more branched and compact structure. While this can result in a higher octane number, it is not as effective as reforming in producing high-octane fuels.

questions.text: Dimethyl ether and ethyl alcohol are examples of ……………….isomerism

questions.topic: Introduction to Fundamental Concepts of Chemistry

options.explanation: Position isomers are constitutional isomers with the same carbon skeleton and will have the same functional group. They differ from each other in the position of the functional groups on the carbon chain.
Chain isomerism are the type of isomerism in which moleculer formula is the same but the arrangement of the carbon chain is different.

Functional Isomerism is the type of isomerism formed by compounds that have same molecular formula but different functional groups. Dimethyl ether and ethanol are said to be isomers that they are different compounds with different functional groups (the -O- ether group and the -OH- alcohol group).
Metamerism are the isomerism having the same molecular formula but different alkyl groups on two sides of functional groups.

questions.explanation: Functional group Functional Isomerism is the type of isomerism formed by compounds that have same molecular formula but different functional groups. Dimethyl ether and ethanol are said to be isomers that they are different compounds with different functional groups (the -O- ether group and the -OH- alcohol group). Position isomers are constitutional isomers with the same carbon skeleton and will have the same functional group. They differ from each other in the position of the functional groups on the carbon chain. Chain isomerism are the type of isomerism in which moleculer formula is the same but the arrangement of the carbon chain is different. Metamerism are the isomerism having the same molecular formula but different alkyl groups on two sides of functional groups. Reference:Sindh textbook

questions.text: Ethers show the phenomenon of:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Position isomerism are constitutional isomers with the same carbon skeleton and will have the same functional group. They differ from each other in the position of the functional groups on the carbon chain.

Functional group isomers are structural isomers that have the same molecular formula ,but the atoms are connected in different ways so that the groupings are dissimilar.These group of atoms are called functional group isomerism.
Metmerism is commonly found in ethers.In this isomerism,the nature of alkyl groups may differ.This occurs due to the unequal distribution of carbon atoms on either side of the functional groups.
Cis trans isomerism also known as geometric isomerism or configurational isomerism, is a term used in chemistry that concerns the spatial arrangement of atoms within molecules.

questions.explanation: Metmerism is commonly found in ethers.In this isomerism,the nature of alkyl groups may differ.This occurs due to the unequal distribution of carbon atoms on either side of the functional groups.

questions.text: Which of the following class of organic compounds contains a ring which has more than one kind of atom?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Aromatic compounds are also cyclic compounds, but they all contain a benzene ring. Benzene is a six-membered ring of carbon atoms with alternating single and double bonds, so they are not hetrocyclic compounds.

The compounds in which the ring consists of atoms of more than one kind are called heterocyclic compound or heterocycles. In heterocyclic compounds generally one or more atoms of elements such as N, O or S which are known as hetero atoms. e.g. Pyridine, Furan, Pyrrole and Thiophene areheterocyclic compounds.
Non-benzenoid aromatic compounds are cyclic compounds that do not contain a benzene ring. Some examples of non-benzenoid aromatic compounds include azulene and naphthalene.

Aliphatic compounds are organic compounds that do not contain any rings. They are typically made up of long chains of carbon atoms with hydrogen atoms attached. Some examples of aliphatic compounds include alkanes, alkenes, and alkynes.

questions.explanation: Heterocyclic compounds are cyclic compounds in which one or more of the ring atoms are elements other than carbon. These other atoms, such as nitrogen, oxygen, or sulfur, are referred to as heteroatoms.

questions.text: Which of the following is general formula of ether?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This is the correct option. The general formula for ethers is R-O-R', where R and R' are alkyl or aryl groups.
This is incorrect. This formula does not represent ethers and instead is a combination not typically used in organic chemistry.
This is incorrect. This is the general formula for aldehydes, not ethers.
This is incorrect. This formula is more representative of esters, not ethers.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A: R-O-R'. Ethers are characterized by an oxygen atom bonded to two alkyl or aryl groups, symbolized by R and R'. Option B, (Cn + H2n+1) CO, is not related to ethers and does not represent any typical organic structure. Option C, CnH2n+1CHO, represents aldehydes, which have a carbonyl group. Option D, (Cn H2n+1 COOCn H2n+1), represents esters, which contain a carbonyl group adjacent to an ether linkage.

questions.text: The compounds in which similar groups are present on the same side of a double bond are called as _?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Trans isomers are characterized by similar groups being located on opposite sides of a double bond. Therefore, this option is incorrect.

Positional isomers refer to compounds that differ in the position of a functional group or a substituent on the same carbon chain. They do not specifically relate to the arrangement of groups around a double bond, making this option incorrect.

Cis isomers have similar substituent groups located on the same side of a double bond. This configuration influences the properties of the compound, distinguishing it from trans isomers, where the groups are on opposite sides. This option is correct.
This option is incorrect because option C is the correct answer.

questions.explanation: In geometric isomerism, the prefix "cis-" (from Latin, meaning "on this side") is used to describe the isomer where two similar or identical substituent groups are on the same side of the double bond or ring.

questions.text: Which of the following does not have sp2 hybridized orbitals?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Acetone has a carbonyl group, which is a carbon-oxygen double bond. The carbon atom in the carbonyl group is sp2 hybridized, with one sigma bond to oxygen and two sigma bonds to hydrogen atoms.
Acetonitrile has a carbon-nitrogen triple bond. The carbon atom in the triple bond is sp hybridized, with one sigma bond to nitrogen and two pi bonds to nitrogen.

Acetic acid also has a carbonyl group, so the carbon atom in the carbonyl group is sp2 hybridized. The other carbon atom in acetic acid is sp3 hybridized, with three sigma bonds to hydrogen atoms and one sigma bond to the carbonyl carbon.

Acetamide has a carbonyl group and an amide group. The carbon atom in the carbonyl group is sp2 hybridized, and the nitrogen atom in the amide group is sp2 hybridized. The other carbon atom in acetamide is sp3 hybridized, with three sigma bonds to hydrogen atoms and one sigma bond to the carbonyl carbon.

questions.explanation: Acetonitrile has a carbon-nitrogen triple bond. The carbon atom in the triple bond is sp hybridized, with one sigma bond to nitrogen and two pi bonds to nitrogen.

questions.text: 2-Pentene shows which type of diastereoisomerism?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: 2-Pentene does not show optical isomerism as it does not have a chiral carbon.

2-Pentene shows geometric isomerism. As it has a double bond, and both carbons have a different group attached to them. One carbon will have hydrogen and a methyl group, while the other has hydrogen and an ethyl group.
The option of optical isomerism is incorrect. Hence, this option is incorrect.
As the option geometric isomerism is correct this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: 2-Pentene shows geometric isomerism. As it has a double bond, and both carbons have a different group attached to them. One carbon will have hydrogen and a methyl group, while the other has hydrogen and an ethyl group.

questions.text: Organic compounds in which tetravalency of carbon atom is satisfied are called as _ ?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Organic compounds in which the tetravalency of the carbon atom is satisfied are called saturated compounds.Saturated compounds have carbon atoms that form four single covalent bonds, either with other carbon atoms or with hydrogen atoms. This structure allows these molecules to hold the maximum number of hydrogen atoms. Alkanes, such as methane (CH4) and ethane (C2H6), are prime examples of saturated hydrocarbons, characterized by their single carbon-carbon bonds. Their general formula is CnH2n+2.

Unsaturated compounds do not satisfy the tetravalency of carbon fully due to the presence of double or triple bonds. These compounds can hold fewer hydrogen atoms compared to saturated ones, making them reactive.

Alkenes are a specific type of unsaturated compounds that contain at least one carbon-carbon double bond. While they do not satisfy the tetravalency of carbon fully, they are not the correct answer to the question regarding saturated compounds.

Alkynes are also unsaturated hydrocarbons characterized by at least one carbon-carbon triple bond. Similar to alkenes, they do not satisfy the tetravalency of carbon, making them incorrect in the context of saturated compounds.

questions.explanation: A saturated organic compound is one in which all the carbon atoms are bonded to the maximum possible number of other atoms (four) via single bonds. This means there are no double or triple bonds present in the molecule.

questions.text: Which one of the following is a cyano group?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: A SH group, also known as a sulfhydryl group, is a functional group that consists of a sulfur atom bonded to a hydrogen atom. The sulfur atom in the SH group is sp3-hybridized. The SH group is often abbreviated as SH.
This is the carboxylic acid group.

The name "cyano" comes from the Greek word "κυανος," which means "dark blue." This is because cyanides, which are compounds that contain the cyano group, are often blue in color. The cyano group is a functional group that consists of a carbon atom triple-bonded to a nitrogen atom. The carbon atom in the cyano group is sp-hybridized, and the nitrogen atom is sp2-hybridized. The cyano group is often abbreviated as -CN.
This is the ester group.

questions.explanation: The name "cyano" comes from the Greek word "κυανος," which means "dark blue." This is because cyanides, which are compounds that contain the cyano group, are often blue in color. The cyano group is a functional group that consists of a carbon atom triple-bonded to a nitrogen atom. The carbon atom in the cyano group is sp-hybridized, and the nitrogen atom is sp2-hybridized. The cyano group is often abbreviated as -CN.

questions.text: The minimum number of carbon atoms present in an organic compound to be able to show position isomerism is_:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: There should be minimum 3 carbon atoms in a compound to show position isomerism.
There should be minimum 3 carbon atoms in a compound to show position isomerism.
There should be minimum 3 carbon atoms in a compound to show position isomerism.
There should be minimum 3 carbon atoms in a compound to show position isomerism.

questions.explanation: There should be minimum 3 carbon atoms in a compound to show position isomerism.

questions.text: Isomerism that arises out of the difference in spatial arrangement of atoms or groups about the doubly bonded carbon atoms are called? (In specific)
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Structural isomerism is defined as molecules that have the same moleculer, but have a different arrangement of the atoms in space.

Stereo isomerism or spatial isomerism is a form of isomerism in which molecules have the same molecular formula and sequence of bonded atoms, but differ in three dimensional orientations of their atoms in space.

The restricted rotation of an atom or groups around the doubly bonded carbon gives different geometries of a compound.Such compounds are called geometrical isomers.They differ in spatial arrangement from one another. Geometrical isomerism are also called as cis-trans Isomerism.

Optical isomerism is a form where the isomers display identical characteristics in terms of molecular weight as well as chemical and physical properties.However ,they differ in their effect on the rotation of polarized light.

questions.explanation: The restricted rotation of an atom or groups around the doubly bonded carbon gives different geometries of a compound.Such compounds are called geometrical isomers.They differ in spatial arrangement from one another. Geometrical isomerism are also called as cis-trans Isomerism.

questions.text: Anthracene contains_number of fused benzene rings.
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. Anthracene consists of multiple benzene rings fused together, and one ring does not adequately represent its structure.
This option is incorrect. Anthracene contains more than two fused benzene rings, specifically three, which is essential for its identity as a polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon.
This is the correct answer. Anthracene is made up of three fused benzene rings, which allows it to exhibit unique chemical properties associated with polycyclic aromatic compounds.
This option is incorrect. Anthracene does not contain four fused benzene rings; it only consists of three, which is critical for recognizing its structure.

questions.explanation: Anthracene is a polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon consisting of three benzene rings fused together in a linear arrangement. Its chemical formula is C14H10.

questions.text: The IUPAC name of isopropyl alcohol is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. 1-butanol is a four-carbon alcohol, whereas isopropyl alcohol has only three carbon atoms.
The IUPAC name of isopropyl alcohol is propan-2-ol, indicating it is a three-carbon chain alcohol with the hydroxyl group (-OH) attached to the second carbon atom.
This option is incorrect. 1-propanol has the hydroxyl group attached to the first carbon, not the second as in isopropyl alcohol.
This option is incorrect. 2-butanol has four carbon atoms, while isopropyl alcohol has only three.
This option is incorrect. 'Propanol' is not specific enough as it doesn't indicate the position of the hydroxyl group, which is crucial in IUPAC naming.

questions.explanation: The correct IUPAC name for isopropyl alcohol is propan-2-ol. This name reflects the structure of the molecule: a three-carbon chain (propane) with a hydroxyl group (-OH) attached to the second carbon atom. The prefix 'iso-' in isopropyl indicates the branching at the second carbon, which aligns with the IUPAC naming as 'propan-2-ol'.Option A (1-butanol) and Option D (2-butanol) are incorrect because they describe four-carbon alcohols, not three. Option C (1-propanol) is incorrect because the hydroxyl group is on the first carbon, not the second. Option E (propanol) is too vague as it doesn't specify the position of the hydroxyl group.

questions.text: When n-butane is heated in the presence of AlCl3 / HCl it will be converted into:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: When n-butane is heated in the presence of AlCl3/HCl, it will be converted into Isobutane.
When n-butane is heated in the presence of AlCl3/HCl, it will be converted into Isobutane.
When n-butane is heated in the presence of AlCl3/HCl, it will be converted into Isobutane. As the reactant is an alkane, it will be converted to an alkane.
When n-butane is heated in the presence of AlCl3/HCl, it will be converted into Isobutane.

questions.explanation: When n-butane is heated in the presence of AlCl3/HCl, it undergoes a rearrangement reaction to form Isobutane.Option A (Ethane), Option B (Propane), and Option C (Butene) are incorrect because the reaction of n-butane with AlCl3/HCl specifically leads to the formation of Isobutane due to the rearrangement of the carbon atoms.

questions.text: Dehydration of alcohols with conc. H2SO4 at 180°C gives:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Ethers are typically formed when alcohols are dehydrated at lower temperatures around 140°C with concentrated H2SO4, not at 180°C.
Esters are formed through esterification reactions, which involve alcohols and acids, but not through dehydration with concentrated H2SO4 at high temperatures.
Alkenes are formed from alcohols through a dehydration reaction at 180°C with concentrated H2SO4. This process involves the removal of a water molecule and the formation of a double bond.
Alkyl halides are typically formed by reactions of alcohols with hydrogen halides, not by dehydration with concentrated sulfuric acid.

questions.explanation: The acid-catalyzed dehydration of alcohols at high temperatures (around 180°C) favors an intramolecular elimination reaction. This process removes a molecule of water and forms a pi bond, resulting in an alkene. At lower temperatures, an intermolecular reaction to form an ether is favored.

questions.text: What is required other than anhydrous AICI3 when toluene is prepared by Friedal craft reaction?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This option is incorrect as toluene cannot be prepared solely with benzene.
Correct! Toluene is prepared by Friedel-Crafts reaction using benzene and CH3Cl along with anhydrous AlCl3.
This option is incorrect as it does not represent the reactants required for toluene synthesis.
This option is incorrect as it includes the wrong reactants for toluene preparation.

questions.explanation: In the Friedel-Crafts reaction for toluene synthesis, anhydrous AlCl3 acts as a catalyst and requires the presence of benzene and CH3Cl. This combination leads to the formation of toluene. The other options provided do not include the correct combination of reactants necessary for the preparation of toluene via this reaction.

questions.text: IUPAC name of this is (CH3)2 - CH - CH (C2H5) - C(CH3)3 :
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This is the correct IUPAC name as it correctly identifies the longest carbon chain and the positions of the substituents.
This name incorrectly places the ethyl group at position 4 instead of 3, which does not follow the IUPAC naming rule of assigning the lowest locant numbers to substituents.
This option incorrectly places the ethyl group at position 5, which is not possible as it is not the smallest locant for the ethyl group.
This name incorrectly places the ethyl group at position 2, which does not reflect the actual structure of the compound.
This option incorrectly places the ethyl group at position 1, which would not be correct as it does not match the structure of the compound.

questions.explanation: The IUPAC name for the given compound (CH3)2-CH-CH(C2H5)-C(CH3)3 is "3-ethyl-2,2,4-trimethylpentane." Here's how the name is derived:1. Identify the longest continuous carbon chain, which in this case is a five-carbon chain.2. Number the carbon chain to give the substituents the lowest possible locants. In this case, we start numbering from the end nearest to the ethyl group, so the ethyl group is located on carbon 3.3. Assign substituent names and locants. The methyl groups attached to carbon 2 and carbon 4 are called "2,2-dimethyl" and "2,4-dimethyl," respectively. The ethyl group attached to carbon 3 is named "ethyl."4. Combine all the parts to form the complete IUPAC name: "3-ethyl-2,2,4-trimethylpentane."Therefore, the compound (CH3)2-CH-CH(C2H5)-C(CH3)3 is named 3-ethyl-2,2,4-trimethylpentane.

questions.text: The common name of 1,3-dihydroxy benzene is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This is not correct because it is 1,3,5-Trimethylbenzene.

The common name of 1,3-dihydroxybenzene is Resorcinol. The other options are different aromatic compounds:Mesitylene: 1,3,5-TrimethylbenzeneDurene: 1,2,4,5-Tetramethylbenzene Anthracene: A polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon with three fused benzene rings
This is not correct because this is 1,2,4,5-Tetramethylbenzene.
This is not correct because it is a polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon with three fused benzene rings.

questions.explanation: The common name of 1,3-dihydroxybenzene is Resorcinol. The other options are different aromatic compounds:Mesitylene: 1,3,5-TrimethylbenzeneDurene: 1,2,4,5-Tetramethylbenzene Anthracene: A polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon with three fused benzene rings

questions.text: Benzene reacts with acetyl chloride in the presence of catalyst AICI3 to give:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The reaction of benzene with acetyl chloride(CH3COCl) in the presence of a catalyst like aluminum chloride (AlCl₃) typically results in the formation of Acetophenone (Aromatic ketone), not an aldehyde.
Correct! The reaction of benzene with acetyl chloride(CH3COCl) in the presence of a catalyst like aluminum chloride (AlCl₃) results in the formation of Acetophenone (Aromatic ketone).
The reaction of benzene with acetyl chloride(CH3COCl) typically results in the formation of Acetophenone (Aromatic ketone), not Benzyl Chloride.
The reaction of benzene with acetyl chloride(CH3COCl) typically results in the formation of Acetophenone (Aromatic ketone), not Benzophenone.

questions.explanation: The reaction of benzene with acetyl chloride(CH3COCl) in the presence of a catalyst like aluminum chloride (AlCl₃) is known as the Friedel-Crafts acylation reaction, which leads to the formation of Acetophenone (Aromatic ketone). Acetophenone is the correct product in this reaction. The other options, Aldehyde, Benzyl Chloride, and Benzophenone, are incorrect as they do not represent the product of this specific reaction.

questions.text: Consider the following reaction
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This option represents the products of an oxidation reaction of ethanol, where CH3CHO (acetaldehyde) is further oxidized to CH3COOH (acetic acid).
This option suggests the formation of acetone and acetic acid, which is not a typical product of ethanol oxidation.
This option suggests partial oxidation of ethanol to acetaldehyde without further oxidation to acetic acid.
This option implies a mixture of unreacted ethanol and its oxidation product acetaldehyde, which is not typical for complete oxidation.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A:CH3CHO, CH3COOH. This is because during the oxidation of ethanol, it first forms acetaldehyde (CH3CHO) and then further oxidizes to acetic acid (CH3COOH). Option B is incorrect because acetone (CH3COCH3) is not a product of ethanol oxidation. Option C is incorrect because it suggests only partial oxidation without forming acetic acid. Option D implies a mixture of ethanol and acetaldehyde, which would occur if the reaction was incomplete or stopped prematurely.

questions.text: Pyridine belongs to which class of organic compounds?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Pyridine is an Alicyclic compound further classified unde the category of hetrocyclic. It has an N in its ring.
It is not a hydrocarbon as it has a N in its ring structure other than C and H atoms.
It is a very general answer as alicyclic compounds are further classified into 2 groups.
It is not a homocyclic compound as it has a N in its ring other than C atoms

questions.explanation: This is the structure of Pyridine.

questions.text: Which of the following is most reactive of all w.r.t electrophilic addition reaction?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Alkanes are the least reactive among the given options due to the absence of any double or triple bonds that can participate in electrophilic addition reactions.
Alkenes are the most reactive of all options because the double bond in alkenes allows for electrophilic addition reactions to occur, making them more susceptible to various types of reactions.
Alkynes are more reactive than alkanes but less reactive than alkenes due to the presence of a triple bond. However, they are still not as reactive as alkenes in electrophilic addition reactions.

Benzene is not the most reactive in terms of electrophilic addition reactions. While benzene does undergo electrophilic aromatic substitution, it is not as reactive as alkenes in electrophilic addition reactions.

questions.explanation: Alkenes are the most reactive of all options in terms of electrophilic addition reactions because the presence of the double bond allows for the addition of electrophiles across the double bond, forming new compounds. Alkanes, alkynes, and benzene are less reactive in comparison due to the absence of or different structures in their chemical compositions.

questions.text: Which of the following species are 3,5 (meta) directing groups when second group is introduced into the benzene ring:I = -NH2 II = -CHO III = -COOH IV = -CH3
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: False! -CHO and -COOH are meta (3,5) directing groups as they are electron withdrawing groups in nature. As they withdraw the electron density from the aromatic ring, they make intermediate carbocations with ortho or para position less stable. Electrophiles attached at meta positions make more stable compounds.

-CHO and -COOH are meta (3,5) directing groups as they are electron withdrawing groups in nature. As they withdraw the electron density from the aromatic ring, they make intermediate carbocations with ortho or para position less stable. Electrophiles attached at meta positions make more stable compounds.

False! -CHO and -COOH are meta (3,5) directing groups as they are electron withdrawing groups in nature. As they withdraw the electron density from the aromatic ring, they make intermediate carbocations with ortho or para position less stable. Electrophiles attached at meta positions make more stable compounds.

False! -CHO and -COOH are meta (3,5) directing groups as they are electron withdrawing groups in nature. As they withdraw the electron density from the aromatic ring, they make intermediate carbocations with ortho or para position less stable. Electrophiles attached at meta positions make more stable compounds.

questions.explanation: -CHO and -COOH are meta (3,5) directing groups as they are electron withdrawing groups in nature. As they withdraw the electron density from the aromatic ring, they make intermediate carbocations with ortho or para position less stable. Electrophiles attached at meta positions make more stable compounds.Option B is correct as -CHO and -COOH are the only groups that are 3,5 directing in this scenario. Option A, C, and D are incorrect as they include groups that are not meta directing, leading to less stable compounds when electrophiles are attached at those positions.

questions.text: The general formula having by alkanes is
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This is incorrect. Alkanes have the molecular formula CnH2n+2, where 'n' represents the number of carbon atoms in the alkane molecule. This formula accounts for all the hydrogen atoms that are present in the molecule.

This is incorrect. This formula doesn't account for the correct number of hydrogen atoms in alkane molecules. Alkanes have two hydrogen atoms per carbon atom, so the formula should include an extra +2 for the hydrogen atoms.
This is incorrect. Alkanes always have an even number of hydrogen atoms per carbon atom. This formula suggests an odd number of hydrogen atoms, which is not consistent with the properties of alkanes.

This is correct. The general formula for alkanes is CnH2n+2. This formula accurately represents the number of carbon and hydrogen atoms in alkane molecules, where 'n' represents the number of carbon atoms.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option D: CnH2n+2. This formula is the general formula for alkanes, where 'n' represents the number of carbon atoms in the molecule. Alkanes have two hydrogen atoms per carbon atom, hence the formula includes +2 for the hydrogen atoms. Options A, B, and C are incorrect because they do not adhere to the correct pattern of alkane molecular formulas, either by omitting hydrogen atoms or introducing an odd number of hydrogen atoms per carbon atom.

questions.text: An optically active compound:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: Must contain at least four carbon atoms.
options.explanation: This is not necessary for a compound to be optically active.

options.text: When in solution rotate the plane of polarized light to the left.
options.explanation: It is not necessary and can rotate the plane of polarized light to either left or right .

options.text: Must always contain asymmetric carbon atom.
options.explanation: It is correct. It should always have an asymmetric Carbon atom and a chiral center.

options.text: In solution always give a negative reading in polarimeter.
options.explanation: It is not necessary as it shows the directon to which the plane polarized light have rotated. So can either be +,_ or 0 0.

questions.explanation: If the compound consists of more than one carbon and contained at least one chiral carbon, it will make the compound optically active. Chiral carbon atoms are also referred to as 'stereogenic carbons' or 'asymmetrical carbon atoms'.

questions.text: Baeyer's reagent is
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This is Baeyer's reagent, which is a dilute alkaline potassium permanganate solution. Alkenes typically react with this reagent to form alcohol.
This combination is used to form alkyl halides and is not Baeyer's reagent.
This option is incorrect as Baeyer's reagent is not represented by this compound. CCl4 is a solvent for alkyl halides.
This option is incorrect as Baeyer's reagent is specifically dilute alkaline KMnO4 solution, not a combination of all the listed options.

questions.explanation: Baeyer's reagent is a dilute alkaline potassium permanganate solution, which is commonly used to test for the presence of unsaturation in organic compounds. Alkenes typically react with this reagent to form alcohol, making option A the correct choice. Options B, C, and D are incorrect as they do not represent Baeyer's reagent and its specific reaction with alkenes.

questions.text: What type of reaction do alkynes undergo across the triple bond
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Alkynes undergo addition reactions across the triple bond because of the weak pi-bonds between carbon atoms that are converted into stronger sigma bonds by the addition of electrophiles.
Alkynes do not typically undergo elimination reactions at the triple bond as they are more prone to addition reactions due to their electron-rich nature.
Alkynes do not typically undergo substitution reactions at the triple bond as they prefer addition reactions to become more stable.
While alkynes can undergo halogenation as an addition reaction, they are not limited to this type of reaction and can also undergo addition reactions with other compounds like water and ammonia.

questions.explanation: Alkynes undergo addition reactions across the triple bond because of their weak pi-bonds between carbon atoms, which are converted into stronger sigma bonds by the addition of electrophiles. This process helps them become more stable. The other options are incorrect because alkynes typically do not undergo elimination or substitution reactions at the triple bond, and while halogenation is a type of addition reaction, alkynes can undergo addition reactions with various other compounds as well.

questions.text: Glyoxal formed by loss of two water molecules by tetrahydroxy ethane oxidizes to
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Incorrect. Glyoxal oxidizes to oxalic acid, not ethylene glycol.
Incorrect. Acetylene is an alkyne, not a product of the oxidation of glyoxal.
Correct! Glyoxal oxidizes to oxalic acid as the product of tetrahydroxy ethane oxidation.
Incorrect. Glyoxal does not have the necessary components to form benzoic acid, which requires a different starting compound.

questions.explanation: When tetrahydroxy ethane oxidizes, it forms glyoxal, which further oxidizes to oxalic acid. This is because oxidation is the addition of oxygen, and the number of carbon atoms remains the same in this process. The other options are incorrect because they do not align with the oxidation products of glyoxal or the starting compound tetrahydroxy ethane.

questions.text: Alkynes are slightly _ in nature.
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Alkynes are slightly acidic in nature due to the presence of a hydrogen atom attached to a highly electronegative carbon-carbon triple bond (sp hybridized) that leads to a polarization of the sigma bond, making the hydrogen atom more susceptible to ionization.

Alkynes are not basic as they do not readily accept protons or donate electrons to act as a base. They are acidic in nature due to the presence of a sigma bond with a hydrogen atom attached to the carbon to carbon triple bond.
Alkynes exhibit acidic properties due to presence of a sigma bond with a hydrogen atom attached to the carbon to carbon triple bond.
Alkynes exhibit acidic properties due to presence of a sigma bond with a hydrogen atom attached to the carbon to carbon triple bond.

questions.explanation: Alkynes are slightly acidic in nature due to the presence of a hydrogen atom attached to a highly electronegative carbon-carbon triple bond (sp hybridized) that leads to a polarization of the sigma bond, making the hydrogen atom more susceptible to ionization. Option B is incorrect because alkynes are not basic and do not readily accept protons. Option C is incorrect as alkynes exhibit acidic properties. Option D is incorrect as alkynes do exhibit acidic behavior.

questions.text: When propyne is treated with dilute H2SO4 in presence of HgSO4 the major product will be:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Propanal is an aldehyde, but alkynes treated with acidified HgSO4 usually form ketones. The major product in this case will be a ketone.
Propyl Hydrogen Sulfate is not the major product in this reaction. The presence of HgSO4 favors the production of a ketone, not a sulfate compound.
When propyne is treated with acidified HgSO4, the major product formed is Propanone, also known as acetone. This is because alkynes (except Ethyne) typically form ketones under these conditions.
Propanol is an alcohol, but alkynes treated with acidified HgSO4 usually form ketones. The major product in this case will be a ketone, not an alcohol.

questions.explanation: When propyne is treated with acidified HgSO4, the major product formed is acetone (Propanone). This is because alkynes (except Ethyne) typically form ketones under these conditions. Propanal, Propyl hydrogen sulfate, and Propanol are incorrect because alkynes react with acidified HgSO4 to form ketones, not aldehydes, sulfates, or alcohols.

questions.text: Acetylene is oxidised by KMnO4 to form
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Acetic acid is not formed from the oxidation of ethyne. It is important to note that oxidation of ethyne results in the formation of diketones, which can further oxidize to produce compounds with carboxyl groups.

Ethyl alcohol is not the product of the oxidation of alkynes. Alkynes typically form carboxylic acids upon oxidation, while alcohols are formed through other reactions such as the addition of water to alkenes.

The correct answer is Oxalic acid. Upon oxidation, ethyne forms vicinal diketones which then further oxidize into carboxylic acids. In the case of ethyne, the final product is Oxalic acid, making this the correct option.

Ethylene glycol cannot be formed from the oxidation of alkynes. The oxidation of alkynes leads to the formation of diketones followed by carboxylic acids, while ethylene glycol is a dihydroxy alcohol and is not the product of this specific oxidation process.

questions.explanation: The end product of the oxidation of ethyne is Oxalic acid. When ethyne undergoes oxidation, it first forms Glyoxal, which then further oxidizes to Oxalic acid. This makes Option C, Oxalic acid, the correct answer. Options A, B, and D are incorrect because they do not align with the specific products formed from the oxidation of alkynes.

questions.text: Butyne and 2-Butyne can be identified by using which of the following reagents?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Oxygen will carry out addition reaction with them, but it is not specific enough to differentiate between butyne and 2-butyne. This option does not aid in the specific identification process of the two compounds.

Br2 will carry out addition reaction with both compounds, but it does not aid in the specific identification of butyne and 2-butyne. This option does not provide a method for distinguishing between the two compounds.

1-Butyne and 2-butyne are distinguished by NaNH2 because 1-butyne reacts with NaNH2 due to the presence of terminal hydrogen. This reagent specifically helps in identifying the differences between butyne and 2-butyne.

HCl is not directly used for the identification of specific compounds like butyne and 2-butyne. Specialized analytical techniques are necessary for accurate identification. This option is not relevant for differentiating between the two compounds.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C: NaNH2. 1-Butyne and 2-butyne can be differentiated using NaNH2 due to the reactivity of terminal hydrogen. Option A and Option B do not provide specific methods for distinguishing between the two compounds, while Option D is not relevant for this identification process.

questions.text: Chlorination of toluene in the presence of sunlight produces
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Chlorination of toluene in the presence of sunlight leads to the substitution of chlorine in the methyl group, forming chloromethyl benzene, also known as Benzyl Chloride.
Benzoic acid is not a product of Chlorination reaction. It can be formed from toluene by oxidation with alkaline KMnO4 followed by acidification.
While p-chlorotoluene can be formed from toluene using Friedel Krafts reaction, in the presence of sunlight, chlorination favors substitution in the methyl group.
Similar to p-chlorotoluene, o-chlorotoluene can be formed through Friedel Krafts reaction. However, in the presence of sunlight, substitution occurs in the methyl group.

questions.explanation: Chlorination of toluene in the presence of sunlight leads to the formation of chloromethyl benzene, also known as Benzyl Chloride. This is because in free radical substitution reactions, the chlorine is substituted in the most reactive position, which is the methyl group in this case. Benzoic acid, p-chlorotoluene, and o-chlorotoluene are not the products of this specific reaction.

questions.text: The resonance energy of benzene is
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This is the correct option as it represents the approximate value of the resonance energy of benzene in the standard unit of kilojoules per mole (kJ/mol).

Cal/mol is not a commonly used unit for measuring energy in chemistry. The standard unit for energy in the International System of Units (SI) is the joule (J) or its multiples, such as the kilojoule (kJ).
The value of 250.5 kJ/mol is not in line with the typical range for benzene's resonance energy. This is not the commonly accepted resonance energy of benzene.

Cal/mol is not a commonly used unit for measuring energy in chemistry. The standard unit for energy in the International System of Units (SI) is the joule (J) or its multiples, such as the kilojoule (kJ).

questions.explanation: Resonance energy refers to the stabilization energy associated with the delocalization of electrons in a molecule. The correct option is 150.5 kJ/mol as it represents the approximate value of the resonance energy of benzene in the standard unit of kilojoules per mole (kJ/mol). Option B and D are incorrect because they use the non-standard unit of calories per mole (Cal/mol). Option C is incorrect as the value provided is not in line with the typical range for benzene's resonance energy.

questions.text: In Chlorination of benzene, which of the following acts as an electrophile?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The correct answer is Cl+, which is the chloronium ion formed in the presence of a Lewis acid during the chlorination of benzene.
Cl- is the chloride ion, which is not an electrophile and cannot react with benzene.
Cl is the chlorine atom, which is neutral and not an electrophile in the context of benzene chlorination.
FeCl3 is a Lewis acid that helps generate electrophiles but is not an electrophile itself in this reaction.

questions.explanation: In the chlorination of benzene, the electrophile is the chloronium ion (Cl+), which is formed in the presence of a Lewis acid like FeCl3. The chloronium ion attacks the benzene ring, replacing a hydrogen atom and forming chlorobenzene. The other options, Cl-, Cl, and FeCl3, are not electrophiles and do not participate directly in the reaction.

questions.text: Which of the following groups are most likely to be o, p directing
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Deactivating groups are less likely to be ortho, para directing. Deactivating groups are electron-poor and withdraw electrons from the benzene ring, making it less reactive.
Neutral groups do not significantly affect the electron density of the benzene ring. They are neither activating nor deactivating.
Activating groups are electron-rich and likely to donate electrons to the benzene ring, making them ortho, para directing.
Only option C is correct as it correctly identifies activating groups as ortho, para directing.

questions.explanation: Activating groups are most likely to be ortho, para directing as they donate electrons to the benzene ring, increasing its reactivity. Deactivating and neutral groups do not have this effect, making them less likely to be o, p directing.

questions.text: Pick the correct one:
questions.subject: English

options.text: How has the chair leg broken?
options.explanation: This option is grammatically correct, but it sounds a bit awkward. While it's understandable, it is less commonly used in standard English compared to the correct option.

options.text: How has the leg of the chair broken?
options.explanation: This is the correct option. It follows standard English syntax and clearly specifies which leg is being referred to (the leg of the chair). The phrase maintains proper word order and clarity.

options.text: How the leg of the chair has broken?
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because it lacks an auxiliary verb to form a proper question. The correct question form requires the auxiliary verb "has" to precede the subject.

options.text: How the chair's leg is broken?
options.explanation: This option is also incorrect for a similar reason as option three. It uses the present tense "is broken" instead of the perfect tense needed to inquire about a past event.

questions.explanation: This is the correct option. It follows standard English syntax and clearly specifies which leg is being referred to (the leg of the chair). The phrase maintains proper word order and clarity.

questions.text: Choose the correct option.We felt as if the ground was _ beneath our feet.
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: The word digging means to make a hole or remove soil using hands or tools.In this sentence, it doesn’t make sense because the ground itself cannot “dig.”It implies an active process done by a person or machine, not something you feel.The phrase “ground digging beneath our feet” sounds unnatural and confusing.It doesn’t convey the idea of instability or movement.Therefore, this word doesn’t fit the emotional or physical sense of the sentence.Hence, digging is not the correct choice here.

The word sinking means going down or being swallowed by something like water or sand.It could slightly fit if the sentence described the ground collapsing or moving downward.However, the expression “felt as if the ground was sinking” implies gradual descent, not sudden instability.It doesn’t match the idiomatic sense of losing balance or stability.“Sinking” conveys heaviness or hopelessness rather than slipping or shaking.Thus, it doesn’t fully capture the intended feeling of unease.So, sinking is not the most accurate word here.

The word slipping perfectly fits the context of this expression.It gives the feeling that the ground is moving or becoming unstable beneath your feet.This suggests uncertainty, fear, or loss of control — both physically and emotionally.It’s a common idiomatic phrase used to describe instability or insecurity in life.For example, when situations change suddenly, people say, “The ground is slipping beneath our feet.”It paints a vivid image of losing footing or confidence.Hence, slipping is the correct and most appropriate option.

The word bursting means exploding, breaking open, or suddenly releasing pressure.It doesn’t make sense in the phrase “the ground was bursting beneath our feet.”While it could describe an earthquake or eruption, the sentence here expresses feeling, not action.The idiomatic tone of the sentence calls for instability, not destruction.“Bursting” feels too violent and literal for this emotional expression.It suggests an outward explosion rather than a subtle movement.Thus, bursting is incorrect in both meaning and usage.

questions.explanation: This is an informal expression that means feeling unstable or losing one’s footing. It’s often used metaphorically to describe a situation where things are unpredictable or uncertain. In the context of the sentence, it conveys the idea that the ground is not stable beneath our feet.

questions.text: A few years ago, it _ that human beings do not belong to this earth.
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: The word found is the simple past form of find, meaning to discover something.However, using found alone here makes the sentence grammatically incomplete.“It found that…” is incorrect because “it” cannot perform the action of finding in this context.The sentence needs a passive construction since the subject “it” receives the action.Also, found alone implies an active voice, which doesn’t suit the meaning here.We are talking about something that was discovered, not something that did the discovering.Therefore, found is not the correct choice.

The phrase was found is in the past tense and passive voice, which perfectly fits this sentence.The subject “it” is singular, so the singular verb “was” agrees correctly.The time phrase “a few years ago” clearly indicates past tense.Thus, “it was found” means “it was discovered” or “it came to be known.”This makes the sentence smooth and meaningful: “A few years ago, it was found that human beings do not belong to this earth.”The structure is grammatically correct and conveys a sense of revelation or discovery.Hence, was found is the correct and appropriate option.

The phrase were found is also in the past tense but plural in form.Since the subject “it” is singular, were cannot be used here.Using were would make the sentence grammatically incorrect.The helping verb must agree with the singular subject “it.”For example, we say “it was found,” not “it were found.”Although were found works with plural subjects (“they were found”), it does not fit this case.Therefore, were found is not correct here.

The phrase had found is in the past perfect tense and indicates an action completed before another past event.However, there’s no second past event mentioned in this sentence.Using had found here creates confusion and incorrect tense sequencing.Also, “it had found” implies that it actively discovered something, which isn’t logical in this context.If we wanted to use the past perfect in passive voice, it should be “had been found.”Since that’s not given, had found remains grammatically wrong and incomplete.Hence, had found is not the correct choice.

questions.explanation: In the given statement, "A few years ago" represents that the sentence is in past tense, and "it" represents that helping verb should be singular. Option D is incorrect because if we used had, it should should have been followed by "been". Hence, correct option is B.

questions.text: A shoal of fish _ killed by the fishermen.
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: Correct, because "shoal" is a singular noun (referring to a group of fish treated as a single entity), so the singular form "has been" is used.
Incorrect, as "have" is used for plural subjects, but "shoal" is singular.
Incorrect, as "had" is the past perfect tense, which isn't needed here; the action is in the present perfect.
Incorrect without "been." While "has" might be used with singular subjects, it requires "been" to form the correct tense here (present perfect).

questions.explanation: Correct, because "shoal" is a singular noun (referring to a group of fish treated as a single entity), so the singular form "has been" is used.

questions.text: At last the fly was _. The most appropriate word to be filled in is:
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: Correct, as it suggests the fly was caught or ensnared by the spider, which is a common scenario in nature.
Incorrect, as this implies the fly is enjoying or amused by the spider, which doesn’t fit the context.
Incorrect, as it suggests the fly was intentionally welcomed by the spider, which is unrealistic.
Incorrect, as this would imply the spider let the fly go, which doesn’t match the typical predatory behavior of spiders.

questions.explanation: Trapped by the spider is correct, as it suggests the fly was caught or ensnared by the spider, which is a common scenario in nature.

questions.text: Spot the error out of the bracketed words. We try to speak (with) (one another) (but) (convey) _nothing.
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: Incorrect. The correct preposition in this context is 'to.' The phrase should be 'speak to one another.'
Correct. This phrase is correctly used to indicate mutual action between two or more parties.
Correct. 'Convey' is correctly used in its base form, fitting the structure of the sentence.
Correct. 'But' is appropriately used to contrast the two clauses in the sentence.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is with because the phrase 'speak with one another' is not idiomatic in English. The proper expression is 'speak to one another,' which indicates direct communication between people. The other options are correctly used: 'one another' refers to mutual actions, 'convey' is correctly used in its base form, and 'but' effectively contrasts the two clauses.

Starting with 'S', we have 'S' -> 'R' -> 'Q', and so on.So, to find the next term in the sequence, we'll continue this pattern:RQPQRQPOPQThe correct option is:D. QPOPQ

Starting with 'S', we have 'S' -> 'R' -> 'Q', and so on.So, to find the next term in the sequence, we'll continue this pattern:RQPQRQPOPQThe correct option is:D. QPOPQ

questions.text: Read the following statement, assuming everything in it to be true. Then decide which of the given suggested courses of action logically follow and are worth pursuing.Statement: Your toddler drops and breaks a glass by genuine accident.Courses of Action:I. yell at them to be more careful with things made out of glass.II. Punish them to make sure they remember to be extra careful next time.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Yelling teaches fear, not learning. Toddlers don’t have the impulse control or the understanding to benefit from being shouted at; it usually makes them anxious and less likely to learn the intended lesson. Yelling can also harm trust and escalate the situation (crying, upset), so it’s not worth pursuing.

Punishment for an honest accident doesn’t reliably teach safer behaviour and can create shame or fear. Toddlers learn best from calm guidance, repetition, and safe practice. Punishment may make them hide future mistakes or become anxious, so it’s not an appropriate or effective course.

Combining yelling and punishment compounds the problems above: it still doesn’t teach the toddler why glass is dangerous or how to act differently, and it risks emotional harm. So both together are even less justified.

This is the best choice: respond calmly, keep everyone safe, and turn it into a simple learning moment.Quick, practical alternative steps you can take right away.Ensure safety first: remove any child from the area and clear up broken glass yourself (or while they watch from a safe distance).Comfort the child if they’re frightened; reassure them accidents happen.Give a very short, simple explanation: “Glass can break and hurt—let’s use plastic/cups with care.”Model the right behaviour and supervise: show how to carry cups carefully, and practice with them when appropriate.

questions.explanation: In this situation, it's important to consider the well-being of your toddler and promote a safe learning environment. Let's evaluate the courses of action: I. Yell at them to be more careful with things made out of glass. This course of action involves raising your voice at the toddler. While it may express your frustration, it's not a constructive way to handle the situation, especially if the child's action was a genuine accident. It may lead to fear and anxiety in the child. II. Punish them to make sure they remember to be extra careful next time. - Punishing a toddler for a genuine accident is generally not recommended. It can create fear and stress, and it's not an effective way to teach them about accidents and safety. It's important to remember that toddlers are still learning and exploring their environment. Given the context of the situation, the better course of action is to: D. Neither I nor II

questions.text: Read the following statements and identify the best cause and effect relation.I. State P started providing multiple rehabilitation schemes, like free education, job search help and help groups for addicts, etc. over the past year.II. Crimes rates for state P have steadily decreased over the past year.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: II is the cause and I is its effect
options.explanation: This option suggests that the decrease in crime rates led to the introduction of rehabilitation schemes, which is illogical as typically, rehabilitation schemes are implemented to reduce crime rates, not as a result of decreased crime.

options.text: I is the cause and II is its effect
options.explanation: This option posits that the implementation of rehabilitation schemes directly caused the decrease in crime rates, which is a logical and plausible explanation.

options.text: I and II are effects of some common cause
options.explanation: This suggests that both the introduction of rehabilitation schemes and the decrease in crime rates stem from a common cause, which is possible but less likely given the direct relationship between rehabilitation and crime reduction.

options.text: I and II are both effects of independent causes
options.explanation: This implies that the rehabilitation schemes and the decrease in crime rates are unrelated and caused by separate factors, which is unlikely given the context of the statements.

questions.explanation: The best cause and effect relation in this scenario is: I is the cause and II is its effect. The implementation of rehabilitation schemes like free education, job search help, and help groups for addicts is likely aimed at reducing crime rates, making it a plausible direct cause of the observed decrease in crime rates in state P.Option A is incorrect because it suggests the opposite causal direction, which is illogical. Option C is less likely because the direct relationship between rehabilitation efforts and crime reduction is well-documented, making a common cause less plausible. Option D is incorrect as it suggests unrelated causes, which is inconsistent with the context provided.

questions.text: Read the passage and the following statements below. Then choose the correct option,basing your answer only on the information provided. Pakistan is rich in wildlife and culture. It is home to many sorts of wildlife,from the Ibex to the Indus River Dolphin ;and people from most countries in the world have made their home here. Statements :i. Pakistan is rich countryii. People from all nationalities of the world live in Pakistan.iii. Pakistan is home to at least one dolphin species.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Let's evaluate each statement based only on the passage:Statement i: "Pakistan is rich country" Incorrect. The passage says "Pakistan is rich in wildlife and culture", not in terms of wealth.Statement ii: "People from all nationalities of the world live in Pakistan." Incorrect. The passage says "people from most countries", not all nationalities.Statement iii: "Pakistan is home to at least one dolphin species." Correct. The passage specifically mentions the Indus River Dolphin, confirming this.Correct answer: Only statement iii is correct.

options.text: Only i and ii are correct
